docs.title: Welcome to the blog, friend
docs.text:
Really glad to have you here. Right now, it really is under construction, there isn't much to see or read. Sure, you'll be able to grasp a glimpse of what the future might hold, but that's all there is to it.

There are two articles I'd like to focus on, three actually:

  • The MetaBlog - Birth

    This first article will describe the adventure I'm currently going through, trying to create my first blog via the, truly, awesome Material for MkDocs.

    Writing In Progress - Barely Started.

  • My NAS Migration

    Yep, that's the one that started all of this. This is the reason I'm starting this blog. It was too painful and not rewarding enough to write this on Reddit. At some point, I lost an evening trying to edit my post to add more to the article I was writing and researching, which ended up not working. I gave up, and here we are

    Writing In Progress - Mostly Done.

  • This Home Page!

    And finally, I'd like to revamp this home page to properly welcome you, give myself another huge list of tasks to make sure my night job will slow me down on my road to becoming a professional Rocket League player.

    What you're seeing here is purely temporary, this needs to be redone entirely.

docs.title: Let's detail the TODOs and measure progress
docs.text:
• Metablog - Birth Article
  • NAS Migration Article
  • Rework this Home page

docs.title: NAS-Related Tags
docs.text: Here's a list of tags used on all articles regarding NAS:

docs.title: The MetaBlog - Birth
docs.text: First article of the metablog, this is exciting. But what the heck is a metablog?

docs.title: What's Meta? Are we talking about Facebook?
docs.text:
As my high-school philosophy teacher taught me, you must always start by defining your terms.

  • HTML for content and structure
  • JavaScript for interactivity
  • CSS for text running out of boxes
  • Internet Explorer ... huh?

docs.title: Welcome to the HomeCinema Project
docs.text: Here we'll be talking about TVs, eARC, AVR, Speakers, Atmos, DTS-X, Dolby Vision, Set-Top boxes etc.

docs.title: Welcome to the HomeLab Project
docs.text: Here we'll be talking about NAS, essentially, but also docker, VMs, hardware, backup strategies, filesystems, torrent, plex, media etc.

docs.title: Welcome to the HomeOffice Project
docs.text:
Here we'll be talking about how to best equipe a home office, from the chair, to the desk.

I'm not sure if everything related to my PC, or future PC plan, and related hardware, and keyboard explorations belong here. They probably deserve their own project, outside of either the HomeOffice or HomeLab projects.

In any case, I will link you back to wherever that will be, whenever it's not strictly about furniture, or printers, scaners, note-taking utilities etc.
